title: clerodendrum cephalanthum subsp. montanum
intro: Clerodendrum cephalanthum subsp. montanum is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the Mountain Glory Bower. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Clerodendrum cephalanthum subsp. montanum include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
